coverage: Manual current-session/current-run events count toward recent_history rules.
Manual events from another session or another run do not count.
Irrelevant in-scope events are part of the working set but do not count as matches.
Policy cache eviction is still property-tested for deterministic created_at_unix_ms then sequence ordering.
Threshold rules do not trigger before enough matching events and trigger once threshold is reached.
Normal /v1/chat/completions requests feed chat_request history before policy evaluation, so a threshold-crossing request can produce a receipt-visible decision.
Literal and regex match conditions are both covered.
Concurrent manual writes to one session/run are serialized by the cache lock and all become visible after writers finish.
Receipts expose scope, count, threshold, matched rule/action, recent_limit, working_set_size, match type, match value, and matched event sequences in policy_actions.
limitations: The prototype stores recent history only in memory and resets it when __test/config is replaced.
recent_history is intentionally scoped only by available session_id and run_id; requests without either identifier do not match recent history.
Automatic chat_request history stores request text in memory for matching, but the external recent-history API redacts that text and marks text_redacted.
Regex patterns use Go regexp syntax and invalid regexes are rejected by __test/config validation.
The test/admin simulation endpoint reports policy decisions but does not create receipts or mutate chat history.
mutation_check: Changing the scope filter to session-only would fail the run isolation assertions.
Counting all in-scope events without literal or regex matching would fail irrelevant-event and regex/literal tests.
Moving chat history insertion after evaluation would fail the second chat receipt threshold test.
Removing cache locking or dropping writes would fail the concurrent visibility test.

self_review.architecture: The implementation extends the existing MemoryPolicyCache and governance rule path instead of adding a parallel history subsystem. This keeps eviction, locking, config reset, and receipt plumbing in one place.
self_review.code_quality: The recent-history rule evaluation is isolated in helper functions. The rule currently returns no action on invalid regex at runtime, but config validation rejects invalid regexes for normal use.
self_review.test_quality: Tests assert behavior through policy results, HTTP handlers, and persisted receipts rather than private fields. Existing property coverage continues to exercise deterministic eviction.
